scenario: An Aurora insurance team adopts Camunda 8.8 DMN tables for 9000 claims per day. A reviewer deploys a decisionTable XML element with no hitPolicy attribute and wants to understand which policy the engine applies by default across the 5 supported policies.
question: Which hit policy applies to a decisionTable element when the hitPolicy attribute is omitted?

options.text: The FIRST hit policy applies by default, returning only the output of the first satisfied rule in the order of rules in the decision table
The UNIQUE hit policy applies by default, requiring at most one satisfied rule and producing the output of that single satisfied rule
The ANY hit policy applies by default, requiring all satisfied rules to produce the same output value before a result is returned
The COLLECT hit policy applies by default, returning the outputs of all satisfied rules as a list in arbitrary order at runtime

a.text: Incorrect. FIRST is one of the five supported hit policies but the docs do not name it as the default; the default applied when hitPolicy is missing on a decisionTable XML element is UNIQUE.

b.text: Correct. The docs state that if no hit policy is set on the decisionTable XML element, then the default hit policy UNIQUE is used, which requires at most one satisfied rule per evaluation.

c.text: Incorrect. ANY is one of the five supported hit policies, but the docs do not designate it as the default when the hitPolicy attribute is absent on the decisionTable element.

d.text: Incorrect. COLLECT is one of the five supported hit policies, but the docs explicitly name UNIQUE as the default applied when no hitPolicy attribute is set on the decisionTable XML element.

explanation: The DMN hit-policy docs state that the hit policy is set in the hitPolicy attribute on the decisionTable XML element and that if no hit policy is set, the default hit policy UNIQUE is used.
